goal: Layer Watchman detection + explicit backend-selection (Notify / Watchman / Poll) + PollWatcher fallback onto the file-watcher module that WO-0026 just landed, flipping P1-W3-F03 to passes=true. Implementation is scoped so unit tests are deterministic on any CI host (watchman binary may or may not be installed).
plan_summary: Extend crates/ucil-daemon/src/watcher.rs with (1) a `WatcherBackend` enum over {NotifyDebounced, Watchman, Poll}; (2) `detect_watchman()` using the `which` crate (new workspace dep); (3) `count_files_capped()` via `walkdir` with early-exit at cap+1 for O(threshold) cost on huge repos; (4) `auto_select_backend()` combining the two to return Watchman iff watchman binary present AND repo file-count >= threshold (otherwise NotifyDebounced); (5) `FileWatcher::new_with_backend(root, sender, backend)` that routes to notify-debouncer (existing path), `notify::PollWatcher` (new Poll path), or a tokio-subprocess Watchman subscription forwarder. `FileWatcher::new` becomes a thin delegate to `new_with_backend(..., NotifyDebounced)` so WO-0026 call-sites stay byte-identical. All tests live at module root under `#[cfg(test)] #[test]` per DEC-0005. The frozen F03 selector `watcher::test_watchman_detection` manipulates `PATH` against a tempdir + fake executable shim for deterministic Some/None outcomes without requiring a real watchman install.
scope_in: Cargo.toml (workspace root) — add `which = "7"` under `[workspace.dependencies]` (cross-platform binary lookup; already widely used in Rust projects incl. cargo/rust-analyzer; the v7 series is current as of 2026-Q1)
crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml — add `which = { workspace = true }` under `[dependencies]`

crates/ucil-daemon/src/watcher.rs — add `pub enum WatcherBackend { NotifyDebounced, Watchman, Poll }` deriving `Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq`. Rustdoc cites master-plan §2 line 138 (notify default, watchman optional upgrade, PollWatcher for network mounts).

crates/ucil-daemon/src/watcher.rs — add `pub struct WatchmanCapability { pub binary: std::path::PathBuf }` deriving `Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq`. No version field yet — `watchman --version` probe is out of scope; a follow-up WO may add it when it becomes load-bearing.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/watcher.rs — add `pub fn detect_watchman() -> Option<WatchmanCapability>` — returns `Some(WatchmanCapability { binary })` iff `which::which("watchman")` returns Ok, else None. `#[tracing::instrument(name = "ucil.daemon.watcher.detect_watchman", level = "debug")]` per master-plan §15.2.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/watcher.rs — add `pub fn count_files_capped(root: &std::path::Path, cap: usize) -> usize` using `walkdir::WalkDir::new(root).into_iter().filter_map(Result::ok).filter(|e| e.file_type().is_file()).take(cap.saturating_add(1)).count()` so the walker stops after at most `cap + 1` entries (caller only needs to know whether the repo is above threshold).

crates/ucil-daemon/src/watcher.rs — add `pub fn auto_select_backend(root: &std::path::Path, threshold: usize) -> WatcherBackend` — returns `WatcherBackend::Watchman` iff `detect_watchman().is_some() && count_files_capped(root, threshold) > threshold`, else `WatcherBackend::NotifyDebounced`. Rustdoc explains the >50K default from master-plan §2 line 138 and notes that Poll is NOT auto-selected (callers opt in for network mounts).
crates/ucil-daemon/src/watcher.rs — add a named const `pub const WATCHMAN_AUTO_SELECT_THRESHOLD: usize = 50_000;` matching master-plan §2 line 138 (>50K files). Export it via lib.rs.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/watcher.rs — add `impl FileWatcher { pub fn new_with_backend(root: &std::path::Path, sender: tokio::sync::mpsc::Sender<FileEvent>, backend: WatcherBackend) -> Result<Self, WatcherError> { ... } }` — dispatches on `backend` to NotifyDebounced (existing body), Poll (new `notify::PollWatcher` variant feeding the same debouncer pipeline OR a simpler direct-forward; implementation detail for executor), or Watchman (new subprocess subscription path).

crates/ucil-daemon/src/watcher.rs — refactor the existing `pub fn new(root, sender)` to `pub fn new(root, sender) -> Result<Self, WatcherError> { Self::new_with_backend(root, sender, WatcherBackend::NotifyDebounced) }` — zero behavior change for WO-0026 callers.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/watcher.rs — Poll backend implementation: construct a `notify::PollWatcher` (notify 6.x exposes `PollWatcher::new` + `Config::with_poll_interval`) and wire its std::sync::mpsc output through the same spawn_blocking forwarder pattern used for the notify-debouncer. Choose a conservative 2-second poll interval and cite it in rustdoc as the network-mount default; make it a named const `PUB const POLL_WATCHER_INTERVAL: std::time::Duration = std::time::Duration::from_secs(2);`.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/watcher.rs — Watchman backend implementation: spawn `watchman --no-spawner --server-encoding=json --json-command` via `tokio::process::Command`, send a `["subscribe", <root>, "ucil-daemon", { "expression": ["allof", ["type", "f"]], "fields": ["name", "type", "exists", "new"] }]` JSON command, parse the JSONL output line-by-line with `serde_json::from_str::<WatchmanEvent>`, forward each file path as a `FileEvent { path, kind: FileEventKind::Modified, source: EventSource::NotifyDebounced }` through the provided `sender`. Use the existing `tokio::task::JoinHandle` forwarder slot on `FileWatcher`. If the subprocess spawn itself fails, return `WatcherError::WatchmanSpawnFailed`.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/watcher.rs — extend `WatcherError` (existing `#[non_exhaustive]` enum at line 153) with new variants: `#[error("failed to spawn watchman: {0}")] WatchmanSpawnFailed(#[from] std::io::Error)` — OR wrap if a From conflict arises with an existing IO arm, prefer a distinct `#[error("watchman spawn: {0}")] WatchmanSpawn(std::io::Error)` without #[from]; `#[error("watchman json decode: {0}")] WatchmanDecode(serde_json::Error)` (add `serde_json = { workspace = true }` to daemon Cargo.toml if not already present — check before adding). Do NOT remove existing variants.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/watcher.rs — add `#[derive(Debug, serde::Deserialize)]` private `struct WatchmanEvent { name: String, exists: bool, new: bool }` used only for JSON decoding in the Watchman backend. Keep visibility `pub(crate)` or lower; not part of public API.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs — extend the existing `pub use watcher::*;` (or equivalent) to cover the new public items: `WatcherBackend`, `WatchmanCapability`, `detect_watchman`, `count_files_capped`, `auto_select_backend`, `WATCHMAN_AUTO_SELECT_THRESHOLD`, `POLL_WATCHER_INTERVAL`. Grouped `pub use` lines per the existing lib.rs style (lines 45-53).

Module-root tests in watcher.rs — ALL at module level under `#[cfg(test)] #[test]` (DEC-0005 precedent, mirroring the 8 existing watcher tests). Required tests (exact names; `test_watchman_detection` is the **frozen F03 selector**):

* `test_watchman_detection` — **FROZEN F03 SELECTOR**. Save existing `PATH` env var; set `PATH` to an empty `tempfile::TempDir`; assert `detect_watchman().is_none()`; create a fake executable file named `watchman` (or `watchman.cmd` + `.exe` on Windows — use `#[cfg(unix)]` gate + a separate `#[cfg(windows)]` stub test if Windows support is in the workspace CI matrix, else skip the cfg gate and let `#[cfg(unix)]` be the default) with mode `0o755` in the tempdir; update `PATH` to include the tempdir; assert `detect_watchman() == Some(WatchmanCapability { binary: <tempdir>/watchman })`; restore `PATH`. Use `std::env::set_var` / `std::env::remove_var` with a `scopeguard`-style cleanup via `let _guard = ...` that runs the restore on drop so the test is failure-safe (inline a tiny `RestorePath` struct if `scopeguard` is not already in deps — do NOT add a new dep for this).
* `test_count_files_capped_below_cap` — create 3 files in a tempdir via `std::fs::File::create`; call `count_files_capped(root, 10)`; assert return is `3`.

* `test_count_files_capped_stops_early` — create 20 files in a tempdir; call `count_files_capped(root, 5)`; assert return is `<= 6` (i.e. walker stopped after `cap + 1`). The point is that the walker early-exits; exact count may be 6 or lower depending on walkdir traversal order.

* `test_auto_select_backend_returns_notify_when_watchman_absent` — set `PATH` to empty tempdir (no watchman shim); call `auto_select_backend(<any tempdir>, 50_000)`; assert returns `WatcherBackend::NotifyDebounced`. Uses the same RestorePath guard.

* `test_auto_select_backend_returns_watchman_when_available_and_above_threshold` — create 11 files in a tempdir; create fake watchman shim in a second tempdir; set PATH to the second tempdir; call `auto_select_backend(<first tempdir>, 10)`; assert returns `WatcherBackend::Watchman`.

* `test_auto_select_backend_returns_notify_when_below_threshold` — create 3 files in tempdir; create fake watchman shim + PATH; call `auto_select_backend(<first tempdir>, 10)`; assert returns `WatcherBackend::NotifyDebounced` (threshold not met).

* `test_poll_backend_delivers_events` — construct `FileWatcher::new_with_backend(&root, tx, WatcherBackend::Poll)?`; spawn a tokio task that writes a file in `root` after 100 ms; await `rx.recv()` with a `tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(10), ...)`; assert the received `FileEvent.path` ends with the expected filename AND `FileEvent.source == EventSource::NotifyDebounced` (Poll events are semantically identical from the consumer's POV — source field remains NotifyDebounced because they flow through the same debouncer pipeline; rustdoc on `WatcherBackend::Poll` must document this).

Rustdoc on every new public item: `WatcherBackend`, `WatchmanCapability`, `detect_watchman`, `count_files_capped`, `auto_select_backend`, `WATCHMAN_AUTO_SELECT_THRESHOLD`, `POLL_WATCHER_INTERVAL`, and `FileWatcher::new_with_backend`. Cite master-plan §2 line 138 (backend selection policy) and §18 Phase 1 Week 3 (watcher feature scope). `#![deny(rustdoc::broken_intra_doc_links)]` is active per .claude/rules/rust-style.md — avoid ambiguous 2-letter method links (past landmine from WO-0009 / WO-0025).
scope_out: NO auto-detection of network mounts — PollWatcher is an explicit opt-in via `WatcherBackend::Poll`; callers (or a future WO) decide when to use it. `/proc/mounts` parsing / `statvfs` network-fs detection is deferred.

NO ucil-cli / `ucil init` wiring — the 'auto-detected at ucil init' language in the feature description is a CLI concern. This WO exposes `auto_select_backend()` so a future CLI WO can call it; this WO does NOT edit `crates/ucil-cli/**`.
NO changes to existing `FileWatcher::new(root, sender)` signature — only the body is refactored to delegate to `new_with_backend(..., NotifyDebounced)`. WO-0026 call-sites must remain byte-identical.
NO changes to `notify_hook_event`, `FileEvent`, `FileEventKind`, `EventSource`, or `DEBOUNCE_WINDOW` — all frozen by WO-0026.
NO new crate, no crate rename, no new `[workspace.members]` entry.

NO watchman subscription integration test in this WO — the unit test covers detection; a future integration-tester WO (run only on hosts with watchman installed via the docker fixture path) can exercise a real subscription end-to-end. Marking any test with `#[ignore]` is FORBIDDEN per root CLAUDE.md.

NO `watchman_client` crate dep — we spawn `watchman` as a subprocess via `tokio::process::Command`. Rationale: avoids a heavy new dep for a feature whose unit test only needs binary detection; subprocess path is also closer to how rust-analyzer invokes watchman. If a future WO requires subscription features that are cumbersome over subprocess (e.g. cookie-based sync primitives), add an ADR at that point.
NO `#[ignore]`, `.skip()`, commented-out assertions, `todo!()`, `unimplemented!()`, `pass`-only test bodies — forbidden by root CLAUDE.md anti-laziness contract.
NO edits to other daemon modules (`lifecycle.rs`, `plugin_manager.rs`, `server.rs`, `session_manager.rs`, `session_ttl.rs`, `storage.rs`, `main.rs`).
NO edits to other crates (ucil-core, ucil-treesitter, ucil-lsp-diagnostics, ucil-embeddings, ucil-agents, ucil-cli, adapters, ml).
NO edits to `ucil-build/feature-list.json`, `tests/fixtures/**`, `scripts/gate/**`, `scripts/flip-feature.sh`, `ucil-master-plan-v2.1-final.md`, or the feature schema.
NO `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { }` wrapper — per DEC-0005, module-root flat tests only. The existing 8 watcher tests follow this pattern.

NO new ADR required — master-plan §2 line 138 unambiguously specifies notify-default / watchman-optional / poll-fallback; detection + backend enum is the minimum viable mechanism. Raise a proposed ADR ONLY if a genuine spec ambiguity or build failure surfaces.

NO relaxation of the PATH-manipulation test safety — if `std::env::set_var` / `remove_var` semantics are unstable across test threads (Rust 2024 marks them as `unsafe` in some stdlib versions), serialize the affected tests via a `std::sync::Mutex<()>` module-level guard rather than reducing assertions.
acceptance: New Watchman-detection + backend-selection API exists in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/watcher.rs`; lib.rs re-exports `WatcherBackend`, `WatchmanCapability`, `detect_watchman`, `count_files_capped`, `auto_select_backend`, `WATCHMAN_AUTO_SELECT_THRESHOLD`, `POLL_WATCHER_INTERVAL` alongside the WO-0026 re-exports.
`cargo nextest run -p ucil-daemon 'watcher::test_watchman_detection'` matches exactly 1 test, 1 passed — the F03 acceptance selector (frozen).

`cargo nextest run -p ucil-daemon 'watcher::'` matches >= 13 tests (8 from WO-0026 + 6 new + test_poll_backend_delivers_events = 15; ≥ 13 floor accounts for possible #[cfg(windows)] gating), all passing.
`FileWatcher::new(root, sender)` still works (WO-0026 backward-compat test, existing `watcher::test_notify_path` or equivalent, unchanged).
`cargo clippy -p ucil-daemon -- -D warnings` clean (no new warnings introduced; clippy::pedantic lints for new code addressed — clippy::module_name_repetitions is already project-allowed).
`cargo doc -p ucil-daemon --no-deps` exits 0 with no `^error:` or `^warning: unresolved link` lines (rustdoc intra-doc links resolve cleanly — past landmine from WO-0009/WO-0025).
Mutation check: `git stash` the body of `detect_watchman()` (replace with `None`) → `test_watchman_detection` fails; `git stash pop` → test passes. (Verifier runs this.)
acceptance_criteria: cd $REPO_ROOT && cargo nextest run -p ucil-daemon 'watcher::test_watchman_detection' --no-fail-fast 2>&1 | grep -E 'tests run: 1, 1 passed' > /dev/null
cd $REPO_ROOT && cargo nextest run -p ucil-daemon 'watcher::' --no-fail-fast 2>&1 | grep -E 'tests run: ([1-9][0-9]+|1[3-9]|[2-9][0-9])' > /dev/null
cd $REPO_ROOT && cargo clippy -p ucil-daemon --all-targets -- -D warnings 2>&1 | tee /tmp/clippy-WO-0027.log && ! grep -q '^error' /tmp/clippy-WO-0027.log
cd $REPO_ROOT && cargo doc -p ucil-daemon --no-deps 2>&1 | tee /tmp/doc-WO-0027.log && ! grep -qE '^(error|warning: unresolved)' /tmp/doc-WO-0027.log
context_refs: master-plan:§2 line 138 (file-watcher selection policy: notify default, watchman >50K files, PollWatcher for network mounts)
master-plan:§18 Phase 1 Week 3 lines 1741-1750 (daemon file-watcher feature scope)
master-plan:§14 lines 1024-1025 (two detection paths — fast-path hook NOT affected by this WO)
rules:.claude/rules/rust-style.md (thiserror per-crate Error enum #[non_exhaustive]; tokio::process::Command in async paths; no unsafe without ADR)
rules:.claude/rules/commit-style.md (~50 LOC per commit soft target; Conventional Commits with Phase/Feature/Work-order trailers)
phase-log:ucil-build/phase-log/01-phase-1/CLAUDE.md (Phase 1 invariants, esp. #3 tokio::time::timeout on all IO awaits and #4 no mocks of critical deps)
predecessor:ucil-build/work-orders/0026-file-watcher-notify-debounce.json (WO-0026 just landed the FileWatcher / FileEvent / EventSource / WatcherError foundation this WO extends)

dependency_readiness_proof.P1-W3-F02: passes=true as of commit 378ecfb (WO-0026 verifier flip). `crates/ucil-daemon/src/watcher.rs` is 638 lines with `FileWatcher`, `FileEvent`, `FileEventKind`, `EventSource`, `WatcherError`, `DEBOUNCE_WINDOW` const all in place — the foundation WO-0027 extends.

lessons_applied: DEC-0005 / WO-0006 / WO-0007 selector-mismatch avoidance: all tests at module root under #[cfg(test)] #[test], matching the 8 existing watcher tests and the quality_pipeline / call_hierarchy precedents.

WO-0009 / WO-0025 rustdoc intra-doc link landmine: acceptance criterion explicitly greps `cargo doc -p ucil-daemon --no-deps` for `^error` or `^warning: unresolved`; rustdoc on new items uses fully qualified paths where ambiguity could arise.

WO-0012 / WO-0020 abandonment pattern: WO-0027 ships with a clear, verifiable acceptance test (`watcher::test_watchman_detection`) whose green path does NOT depend on watchman being installed on the test host — executor / verifier loop cannot get stuck on environment absence.
WO-0024 rustdoc regression: explicit rustdoc gate in acceptance_criteria[3] catches any new unresolved-link warning before verifier spin-up.
